description: Bundle-size budget churn had two root causes: (1) agents budgeting gzip from Vite's build-log reporter, which gzips ~2KB COOLER than scripts/check-bundle-size.ts (Node zlib level 6) — the actual CI gate; (2) stale src/ui/node_modules building a different bundle than CI's fresh install, mistaken for macOS<->Linux drift. Never hand-edit budgets or trust Vite's gzip number.
resolution: build:ui now installs --frozen-lockfile so a clean local build is byte-identical to CI (verified same hash 583998raw/173931gzip); added check-bundle-size.ts --update to re-baseline budgets from the measured build (measured+small headroom), refusing to RAISE unless WARREN_BUNDLE_SIZE_ALLOW_RAISE=1. Budget gzip js set to honest CI floor 174300 (bundle genuinely gzips to 173931 — the framer-motion motion layer — so it could not be ratcheted lower).

content: Pin bun across all GitHub Actions via a repo-root .bun-version file (single source of truth) plus explicit 'with: bun-version-file: .bun-version' on every oven-sh/setup-bun@v2 step (ci.yml, ci-postgres.yml, release.yml, bundle-size-autoheal.yml). Keeps Vite/esbuild minified output — and thus measured bundle-size budgets — reproducible; bump the version deliberately and re-run 'bun run check:bundle-size:build --update' at the same time. Match the version local devs run.
